best_overall_response.evidence: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis after completion of chemoradiation shows decrease in right lower lobe mass from 5.8 cm to 3.2 cm. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy decreased. No distant metastases. Partial response.

response_criteria_used.evidence: Solid tumor (NSCLC adenocarcinoma) assessed by CT with measurable lesion dimensions (5.8 cm to 3.2 cm, ~45% decrease). Terminology 'partial response' and 'stable disease' consistent with RECIST 1.1 criteria. Criteria system not explicitly named in notes.

response_assessment_date.evidence: No specific dates are provided in any of the clinical notes, radiology reports, or progress notes. Reports reference relative timepoints (e.g., '3 months', '6 months', '12 months') but no calendar dates.

duration_of_response_months.evidence: PR documented post-chemoradiation. 'One year post-chemoradiation, 12 months on consolidation osimertinib' indicates osimertinib started at chemoradiation completion. PET/CT at 15 months on osimertinib shows no progression. Duration of response is at least 15 months from first documented response with no progression event.

ecog_ps_at_treatment_start.evidence: ECOG not explicitly stated prior to chemoradiation initiation. First ECOG documented is 'ECOG 1' after chemoradiation completion when starting consolidation osimertinib: 'Recovered well from treatment. ECOG 1. Plan: Start consolidation osimertinib.' Inferred as baseline performance status given clinical context.

ecog_ps_at_best_response.evidence: Best response (PR) was documented on post-chemoradiation CT. The subsequent clinical note states: 'Recovered well from treatment. ECOG 1. Plan: Start consolidation osimertinib 80 mg daily.' This ECOG assessment coincides with the best response timepoint.

review_items.reason: No calendar dates present in any clinical notes; only relative timepoints referenced
ECOG not documented before chemoradiation start; inferred from post-chemoradiation note (confidence 0.60)
RECIST 1.1 inferred from solid tumor context and response terminology but not explicitly named in notes (confidence 0.75)
Not extracted: treatment start date and first response date not available as calendar dates; insufficient precision to calculate
Not extracted: treatment ongoing at last follow-up, no discontinuation event documented
Not extracted: no disease progression documented through 15 months of follow-up
